beats.text: Cleaning verification on e-wizer — the whole loop, start to finish. This is your day: areas covered, checks done, and anything waiting on follow-up. Let's work.
From Home, start with the Scan QR code button in the hero. This is the main way to begin your area check-in.
You can also use the centre scan button in the control panel at any time. It opens the same scanner and follows the same check-in path.
Point the scan frame at the zone QR. Once the area is recognised, e-wizer checks you in and opens that zone.
If work is scheduled for today, it appears under Due. Start there first. These are the checks expected for this zone today.
Sometimes nothing shows under Due. That can happen on a holiday, an extra booked shift, an ad-hoc clean, or a weekly or monthly clean that is not due today.
Open Off-schedule. This is the full zone catalogue for work done outside today's schedule. Capture only what you actually did. Nothing here is forced.
Surface checks out clean? One tap on the green tick. Done. Next.

beats.chip: Found a problem? Tap the cross
beats.text: Found a problem? Tap the red cross — and the remedial action capture opens. This is where the real value lives, so slow down right here.
Photos are optional — but they're gold. Snap the finding, circle the dirt, drop an arrow. Ten seconds now saves an argument later.
Grade it. Minor — one. Major — two. Critical — three. Dried dough caked on a rounder frame? That's a Major.

This is exactly the way you are used to grading findings. One is still Minor: traces on non food contact surfaces, low contamination risk. Two is Major: traces on equipment contact surfaces, high product contamination risk. Dried dough on the rounder frame fits here. Three is Critical: not cleaned, gross soiling, not fit for production.
Now write down what you found, and what happens about it. Then give it an owner. Jabulani is cleaning this one. No owner, no action.
Submit. Recorded, time-stamped, on the record.
The rest of the area checks out clean? You've earned the shortcut. PASS REMAINING — nine checks, one tap.
Confirm — and they're recorded as passes against your name.
Area done. One hundred percent — with the failure honestly on the record. That's not a bad day. That's a credible one.
But hear me — recording a remedial is NOT the end of the job. Back home, it queues on the follow-up banner. And it stays there until somebody closes it.
This is the part you MUST do. Open the queue — every outstanding remedial, graded, with the photo and the owner. Follow up until it's fixed.
When the work's done, go look at it. Add a note — an after-photo if you like — and confirm.
All clear. Found it, fixed it, followed it up. THAT is cleaning verification — not a tick exercise. A closed loop.
